data.acceptance:
1. Explicit `knots-e2e` runs provide a clear, machine-readable or unambiguous prompt signal that the e2e skill controls the workflow boundary across multiple claim states.
2. Agents can continue after `kno next` by reclaiming the knot without being blocked by the previous claim output’s one-action stop instruction.
3. Ordinary non-e2e claims still instruct agents to complete exactly one workflow action and stop.
4. Terminal-state movement remains explicitly authorized only when the user or e2e invocation grants it.
5. Documentation or skill text includes the exact user-facing override wording and explains why it is needed until the workflow provides first-class support.
